name: Individual Beef Wellingtons with Mushroom Sauce
recipeIngredient: 8 Beef rib eye steak, 1 inch thick by 3x5 inches
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove stems from 8 mushrooms and set aside.
2. Mince the remaining mushrooms and mushroom stems.
3. In a large skillet over medium heat, melt butter. Add the minced mushrooms and onions, and cook for 5 minutes or until all liquid is evaporated.
4. Stir in bread crumbs, thyme, salt, and pepper. Cool mixture.
5. Prepare puff pastry according to package directions.
6. Cut 8 rectangular pieces from the pastry, reserving scraps.
7. Place about 1/3 cup of mushroom mixture in the center of each pastry rectangle. Top with a steak and one whole mushroom on each.
8. In a small bowl, beat egg whites and 2 tsp water with a fork until combined.
9. Brush the edges of the pastry around the filling.
10. Fold the pastry over the steak and mushroom, overlapping the edges and pressing to seal.
11. Roll out the reserved scraps, cut into leaf or other designs.
12. Brush the backs of these pieces with egg white.
13. Arrange on a cookie sheet.
14. Refrigerate for 2-3 hours before baking.
15. Let meat sit at room temperature for about 30 minutes before baking.
16. Preheat oven to 400°F.
17. In a cup, beat egg yolk and 2 tsp water. Brush over the pastry.
18. Bake for 23 minutes for rare, or 25 minutes for medium.
This can be served with asparagus tips, baby carrots, and broiled tomato halves.
